answer: a particle made mostly of empty space with almost all its mass concentrated in a very small dense nucleus containing protons and neutrons, with electrons in shells around the nucleus

prompt: Define atomic number / proton number, Z.
answer: the number of protons in the nucleus of an atom
a subatomic particle outside the nucleus with relative charge -1 and very small relative mass
the energy required to remove one electron from each atom in one mole of gaseous atoms to form one mole of gaseous 1+ ions
atoms of the same element with the same number of protons but different numbers of neutrons

prompt: Define mass number / nucleon number, A.
answer: the total number of protons and neutrons in the nucleus of an atom
a subatomic particle in the nucleus with relative charge 0 and relative mass about 1
a subatomic particle in the nucleus with relative charge +1 and relative mass about 1
